studies.description: CT Chest with IV contrast — follow-up after ARDS resolution
studies.preview: Resolution of prior ARDS pattern. STABLE 2.5 cm solid RUL pulmonary nodule with smooth-to-mildly-spiculated borders and homogeneous post-contrast enhancement (~40 HU), unchanged in size from 2/16 CXR.
ACUTE OCCLUSIVE DVT in right common femoral, R superficial femoral, and R popliteal veins (proximal). Right calf veins patent. Left LE no DVT.

Bilateral interstitial markings, small bilateral pleural effusions — consistent with developing ARDS. INCIDENTAL 2.5 cm well-circumscribed opacity in RIGHT UPPER LOBE, newly visualized — recommend dedicated chest CT for further characterization.

studies.description: CT Abdomen + Pelvis with IV contrast
studies.preview: Acute interstitial pancreatitis with peripancreatic stranding. CHOLELITHIASIS with suspected CHOLEDOCHOLITHIASIS — distended GB with multiple stones, 6 mm filling defect in distal CBD, intrahepatic biliary dilatation (CBD 9 mm). Mild GB wall thickening. Recommend urgent ERCP.
Cholelithiasis without acute cholecystitis. Multiple gallstones in distended GB; wall not thickened. No biliary obstruction. CBD 5 mm.
No radiographic evidence of active or healed pulmonary TB. No nodules, masses, or focal infiltrates. Lungs clear bilaterally. (Important historical baseline for comparison.)
